questions.question: What does 'HTTP' stand for?
questions.explanation: HTTP is the foundational request/response protocol of the web.

questions.question: In Git, which command stages all modified and new files?
questions.explanation: 'git add .' stages everything in the current directory tree.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of binary search on a sorted array?
questions.explanation: Each step halves the search space, giving logarithmic time.

questions.question: Which data structure uses FIFO (first-in, first-out) ordering?
questions.explanation: A queue removes elements in the order they were added; a stack is LIFO.

questions.question: What port does HTTPS use by default?
questions.explanation: HTTPS defaults to port 443; plain HTTP uses 80.

questions.question: Which language runs natively in web browsers?
questions.explanation: JavaScript is the browser's native scripting language (others need Wasm).

questions.question: In Big-O notation, which grows fastest as n increases?
questions.explanation: Exponential growth outpaces all polynomial terms.

questions.question: What does 'SQL' stand for?
questions.explanation: SQL is the standard language for relational databases.

questions.question: Which HTTP status code means 'Not Found'?
questions.explanation: 404 signals the requested resource doesn't exist; 200 is success.

questions.question: Which sorting algorithm has the best worst-case time complexity?
questions.explanation: Merge sort guarantees O(n log n); quicksort's worst case is O(n^2).

questions.question: What does 'RAM' stand for?
questions.explanation: RAM is volatile working memory the CPU reads and writes directly.

questions.question: In regex, what does the '\d' metacharacter match?
questions.explanation: '\d' matches a single digit 0-9; '\w' matches word characters.

questions.question: Which company originally developed the Kubernetes project?
questions.explanation: Google open-sourced Kubernetes in 2014, based on its Borg system.

questions.question: What is the default branch name for a newly initialized modern Git repo (Git 2.28+ default config aside)?
questions.explanation: Historically 'master'; configurable via init.defaultBranch, commonly 'main'.

questions.question: What symbol starts a comment in Python?
questions.explanation: Python uses '#' for single-line comments.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin 'aurum'.

questions.question: How many planets are in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Eight, since Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006.

questions.question: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's ATP energy.

questions.question: What is the speed of light in a vacuum (approx)?
questions.explanation: Roughly 299,792 km/s — about 300,000 km/s.

questions.question: Which gas do plants primarily absorb for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants take in CO2 and release oxygen during photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere.

questions.question: What particle carries a negative electric charge?
questions.explanation: Electrons carry a negative charge; protons are positive.

questions.question: What is the hardest known natural material?
questions.explanation: Diamond tops the Mohs hardness scale at 10.

questions.question: Water is made of hydrogen and which other element?
questions.explanation: Water is H2O — two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

questions.question: What is the approximate age of the universe?
questions.explanation: Best estimates from the cosmic microwave background put it at ~13.8 Gyr.

questions.question: Which blood cells help fight infection?
questions.explanation: White blood cells (leukocytes) are the immune system's front line.

questions.question: What force keeps planets in orbit around the Sun?
questions.explanation: Gravity provides the centripetal force holding planets in orbit.

questions.question: What is the pH of a neutral solution at 25°C?
questions.explanation: pH 7 is neutral; below is acidic, above is basic.

questions.question: Which scientist proposed the theory of general relativity?
questions.explanation: Einstein published general relativity in 1915.

questions.question: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: Adults have 206 bones; babies start with about 270 that fuse over time.

questions.question: What is the capital of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo has been Japan's capital since 1868.

questions.question: How many continents are there on Earth?
questions.explanation: The commonly taught model counts seven continents.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile (~6,650 km) is traditionally cited as longest, though the Amazon rivals it.

questions.question: How many sides does a hexagon have?
questions.explanation: 'Hex-' means six.

questions.question: Which language has the most native speakers worldwide?
questions.explanation: Mandarin has the most native speakers; English leads in total speakers.

questions.question: What color do you get by mixing blue and yellow paint?
questions.explanation: Blue and yellow pigments combine to make green.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Iron oxide on Mars's surface gives it a reddish hue.

questions.question: How many colors are in a rainbow (traditional count)?
questions.explanation: Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet.

questions.question: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers about a third of Earth's surface.

questions.question: Which country has the most time zones?
questions.explanation: Counting overseas territories, France spans 12 time zones.

questions.question: How many minutes are in a full day?
questions.explanation: 24 hours × 60 minutes = 1440.

questions.question: What is the currency of the United Kingdom?
questions.explanation: The UK uses the pound sterling (£).

questions.question: Which animal is known as the 'King of the Jungle'?
questions.explanation: The lion, despite mostly living in savannas, wears the title.

questions.question: What is the tallest mountain above sea level?
questions.explanation: Everest reaches 8,849 m above sea level.

questions.question: How many keys are on a standard piano?
questions.explanation: A standard piano has 88 keys — 52 white and 36 black.

questions.question: In which year did World War II end?
questions.explanation: WWII ended in 1945 with the surrender of the Axis powers.

questions.question: Who was the first President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Washington served from 1789 to 1797.

questions.question: Which ancient civilization built the pyramids at Giza?
questions.explanation: The Giza pyramids were built by ancient Egyptians around 2600–2500 BCE.

questions.question: The Great Wall is located in which country?
questions.explanation: The Great Wall of China stretches thousands of kilometers.

questions.question: In which year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.

questions.question: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
questions.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted it in the early 16th century.

questions.question: Which empire was ruled by Julius Caesar?
questions.explanation: Caesar led the Roman Republic as it transitioned toward empire.

questions.question: In which year did the RMS Titanic sink?
questions.explanation: The Titanic sank on its maiden voyage in April 1912.

questions.question: Who is credited with discovering gravity after (legend says) an apple fell?
questions.explanation: The apple story is tied to Isaac Newton.

questions.question: Which document begins with 'We the People'?
questions.explanation: 'We the People' opens the preamble to the U.S. Constitution.

questions.question: The Renaissance began in which country?
questions.explanation: The Renaissance began in Italy in the 14th century.

questions.question: Who was the British Prime Minister during most of World War II?
questions.explanation: Churchill led Britain for most of the war, from 1940.

questions.question: In 1969, humans first landed on the...?
questions.explanation: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon in July 1969.

questions.question: Which war was fought between 1914 and 1918?
questions.explanation: World War I ran from 1914 to 1918.

questions.question: Who wrote the theory of evolution by natural selection?
questions.explanation: Darwin published 'On the Origin of Species' in 1859.
